behaviors.description: The payload sent to summary generation contains data from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module data handoff (three collectors -> aggregation -> summary input), not single-function logic. A wired in-process test with stubbed source clients can catch missing/overwritten section bugs. Rejected Unit because behavior text requires inclusion from "all three sources," which is a module interaction contract. Rejected System because this behavior is about payload composition, not real network/OS effects; real HTTP is not required to expose the handoff failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because it does not assert model decision quality or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run collectors and aggregator together with deterministic fixtures for each source, invoke report assembly up to the summary-generation call boundary, and assert the outgoing summary input contains all three source sections/keys with expected values.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report generation continues and marks that section as data unavailable.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience orchestration across modules (source adapter error -> report composer behavior). A single-function unit test is insufficient for real failure at the adapter/composer boundary. Rejected System: behavior says "one data source is unavailable" but does not require validating real HTTP timeout semantics; simulated adapter failure is enough for the targeted failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model judgment, only fallback control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire source adapters + report builder with one adapter configured to raise an availability error; verify report is still produced, includes a `data unavailable` marker for that section, and other sections are present.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately reflects key values from input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (faithful representation of key values), which is model-output dependent. Stubbed/deterministic responses can mask hallucination or omission failures. Rejected Integration because the behavior is explicitly about semantic accuracy of generated narrative, not routing/retry/payload wiring around generation. Rejected Workflow because this is a single generation-quality check, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against real model/service using representative fixtures; score whether required key values/facts are preserved in the narrative (with acceptance rubric/threshold over repeated runs to account for variance).

behaviors.description: End-to-end report flow completes and email is delivered to configured recipient inbox.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external email delivery and inbox receipt, which requires real HTTP/API and external-system interaction. In-process mocks can hide transport/auth/provider integration failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires "through email delivery" and "reaches ... inbox," a real external boundary. Rejected Agentic: although pipeline may include summary generation, this behavior's pass condition is delivery completion, not model output quality; generation can be fixed/deterministic for this test.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduled job in a test environment with real provider/test mailbox credentials, trigger one full report run, then verify message arrival in the configured inbox with expected subject/body structure.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, report is saved locally and retried on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff/persistence across runs (delivery failure -> local save -> later retry), which can be reproduced by wiring modules with a failing mailer stub plus real local filesystem. Rejected System because behavior does not require validating provider-specific outage semantics; unreachable condition can be represented by deterministic delivery exceptions while still testing true persistence/retry logic. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output correctness or multi-agentic chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use real local filesystem (temp path) and wired scheduler/report modules: first run with mailer stub failing as unreachable, assert fallback file is written; second run with mailer stub recovered, assert stored report is retried/sent and fallback artifact/state is cleared.
